by NATO members
options.c:
A hypersonic air-to-surface weapon tested by
the US Indo-Pacific Command
options.d:
A thermobaric bomb used for area-denial
operations by Iran

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator (MOP) is the largest non-nuclear bomb in the US** **inventory.** Built by Boeing and guided by GPS, it is engineered specifically to target deeply buried and hardened bunkers.
● The MOP measures 20.5 feet in length and 31.5 inches in diameter, and weighs just under 30,000 pounds—including 5,300 pounds of high-explosive material.
● The bomb can burrow more than 200 feet through reinforced concrete before detonating. Its casing, made from high-performance steel alloy, helps it withstand the immense impact forces upon penetration. MOP’s explosive yield is more than 10 times greater than that of its predecessor, the BLU-109. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Recently, the US used its most powerful conventional weapon—the Massive Ordnance Penetrator (MOP), targeting Iran’s underground nuclear facility at Fordow.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Climate Change and Impacts of Climate Change over Earth
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement-I:** Climate change has been driving a rise in the Malaria, Dengue, and Chikungunya cases in recent years. **Statement-II:** Rising temperatures shorten the mosquito breeding cycle and increase their biting rate.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct
and Statement–II is the correct explanation for Statement–I
options.b:
Both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct
and Statement–II is **not** the correct explanation for Statement–I
options.c:
Statement–I is correct but Statement–II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement–I is incorrect but Statement–II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● In India, **climate‑sensitive diseases** like malaria, dengue, chikungunya, scrub typhus, Japanese Encephalitis, and cholera have **become more common** over the past decade. The recent Lancet Countdown 2025 Report and Research by the National Centre for Vector Borne Diseases Control (NCVBDC) shows that **changes in temperature, humidity, and rainfall are driving this trend**. Warmer
weather allows mosquitoes and ticks to spread to new areas. Irregular monsoons create more breeding sites, making it easier for these diseases to spread. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Rising temperatures** **shorten the breeding cycle** of mosquitoes and **increase their biting rate**. Changes in **precipitation**, **relative humidity**, and **urban heat-island effects** also **enhance the survival window** **of vectors** like Aedes aegypti. Warmer nights and extended summers allow pathogens like the dengue virus, Plasmodium parasites to complete their incubation cycles faster within vectors. According to ICMR and WHO findings, even a 1–2°C temperature increase significantly boosts vector transmission efficiency. **So, statement–II is correct**
● The improved survival, breeding, and transmission potential of vectors due to rising temperatures and ecological shifts is exactly what leads to the observed increase in climate-sensitive diseases. **So, both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct and Statement–II is the correct explanation for** **Statement–I** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Recently, the Lancet reported that fossil fuel air pollution caused 1.72 million deaths in India in 2022**.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Rotavirus infection:
I. 	 It spreads mainly through the faeco-oral
route, the same pathway responsible for most acute diarrhoeal diseases in young children.
II. 	 There is no vaccine to prevent Rotavirus
infection.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● Rotavirus is a **contagious gastrointestinal (GI) infection** that causes inflammation of the stomach and intestines. The virus **enters the body through the mouth.** Viral replication occurs in the villous epithelium of the small intestine. Up to two-thirds of children with severe rotavirus gastroenteritis show the presence of rotavirus antigen in serum (antigenemia) and children can have rotavirus RNA detected in serum.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● This can lead to **severe diarrhea and vomiting**. People of all ages can get rotavirus. But it **mostly occurs** **in children younger than 1 year** old.
● The World Health Organization (WHO) has approved several rotavirus vaccines that are safe and effective. These **vaccines**, including **Rotarix, RotaTeq, and Rotavac**, are oral and made from weakened forms of the virus. In 2016, **India introduced Rotavac**, an **indigenous** oral rotavirus vaccine. It was added to the Universal Immunisation Programme. **So, statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: A recent study on the impact of the indigenous rotavirus vaccine in India found marked reductions in rotavirus-based gastroenteritis in sites across the country.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Indian Carbon Market (ICM) and the European Union’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM):
I. 	 Both ICM and CBAM exclusively deal with
carbon taxes levied on imported goods.
II. 	 Under the EU-India Strategic Agenda 2025,
carbon prices paid in India will be deducted from CBAM levies at the European Union border.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● A **Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)** is a fee or tariff levied by the European Union to put “a fair price on the carbon emitted during the production of **carbon-intensive goods that are entering** **the EU** and to encourage cleaner industrial production in non-EU countries.” Indian Carbon Market is a digital platform dedicated to facilitating seamless trading of carbon credits, promoting sustainability, and supporting businesses in their journey towards carbon neutrality. While C**BAM exclusively deals with** **carbon costs on certain imported goods** into the EU, the **ICM is a domestic initiative to reduce** **emissions within India. So, statement 1 is not correct.**
● Recently, the European Union (EU) and India have agreed to explore linking the **Indian Carbon Market** **(ICM)** with the **EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)** as part of the New Strategic EU– India Agenda under the pillar of clean transition.
○ EU law permits the **deduction of a third country’s carbon price** while calculating CBAM liabilities. So, **Carbon prices paid in Indi**a will be **deducted from CBAM levies** at the EU border. **So, statement II is correct.** ○ CBAM recognises carbon charges paid under the ICM. This helps **Indian exporters avoid double** **carbon pricing**. However, India’s current carbon credit prices (~€5–€10) are far lower than EU rates, so the actual deduction may be limited. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: India and the EU are working to connect the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism with India’s carbon market as part of the New Strategic EU‑India Agenda.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.microTopic: Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the UNEP Emissions Gap Report 2025:
I. 	 The world’s largest emitters, China, the US,
the EU, and India, have not met their Paris pledges.
II. 	 Existing national pledges place the world on
a warming trajectory of around 4°C to 5°C by the end of the century.
III. 	Limiting warming to below 2°C, countries
must cut emissions to 37% of 2019 levels by 2035.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● According to the **UNEP Emissions Gap Report 2025**, major emitters, **China, the U.S., the EU, and** **India**, have **not met their Paris pledges**. Many countries are either underachieving or implementing policies too slowly, resulting in a persistent gap between pledged and actual emission reductions. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● Ten years ago, **before** the adoption of the **Paris Agreement**, the world was heading towards **global** **warming** of around **4°C-5°C by the end of the century**. However, due to stronger commitments and collective action **after the Agreement,** the **projected rise** has been reduced to about **2°C–3°C**. **So,** **statement II is not correct.**
● Recently, the United Nations released a **2025 synthesis report**. It shows that to keep temperatures below 2°C and 1.5°C, countries must cut emissions 37% and 57% respectively of 2019 levels by 2035. **So,** **statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The Paris Agreement has once again come into the spotlight as it marks its decade-long anniversary.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With reference to the missiles of India, how does
Dhvani differ from BrahMos?
I. 	 BrahMos is a supersonic cruise missile,
whereas Dhvani is a hypersonic glide vehicle.
II. 	 BrahMos uses a liquid-fuelled ramjet engine,
while Dhvani employs scramjet technology.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Dhvani Missile** is a hypersonic missile being developed by India's **Defence Research and Development** **Organisation (DRDO)**. The Dhvani is being developed as a **Hypersonic Glide Vehicle (HGV),** a revolutionary weapon system that combines blistering **speed** with unprecedented **maneuverability.** The **BrahMos** is a medium-range **supersonic cruise missile**, developed as a joint venture between India and Russia. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Unlike conventional cruise missiles that follow **predictable flight paths,** the Dhvani will be launched to **extreme altitudes** before gliding toward its target at hypersonic speeds.  This unique capability makes it nearly **impossible to detect** and even harder to intercept. It will be capable of striking **both land-based** and **maritime targets** with pinpoint precision.
● **BrahMos** uses a two-stage propulsion system: a **solid booster** for launch and a **liquid-fuelled ramjet** for sustained supersonic cruise. **Dhvani**, building upon India’s **HSTDV programme**, is expected to use **scramjet technology** and hypersonic glide dynamics. **So, statement II is correct.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Key Features of Dhvani Missiles**
● It can fly at **speeds exceeding Mach 5 or 6**, nearly 7,400 km per hour.
● It has an estimated **range** between **6,000 to 10,000 kilometers.**
● The missile features an **Ultra-High-Temperature Ceramic Composite (UHTCC)** heat protection system to withstand the extreme temperatures to **survive** **temperatures between 2,000°C and 3,000°C** generated during atmospheric reentry.
**Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) is planning to conduct the first test flight for its Dhvani hypersonic missile by the end of 2025.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
South-South and Triangular Cooperation:
I. 	 It is a cooperation mechanism where Global
South countries collaborate with support from a developed nation or a multilateral agency.
II. 	 It was established through the Addis Ababa
Action Agenda of 2015.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **South-South and Triangular Cooperation (SSTC)** is a development cooperation modality driven by **countries of the Global South** through mutual exchange of knowledge, technology, expertise, and resources. It is built on **principles of solidarity,** national ownership, **non-conditionality,** equality, and mutual benefit, functioning as a complementary mechanism to traditional North–South aid.
● SSTC involves **two or more developing (Global South) countries**, and Triangular Cooperation includes the **support of a third party**, typically a **developed nation** or **multilateral body.** **So, statement I is** **correct.**
● South-South Cooperation was formally outlined under the **Buenos Aires Plan of Action (BAPA), 1978**, **not the Addis Ababa Action Agenda** (which pertains to Financing for Development, 2015). **So,** **statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** On September 12, 2025 (UN Day for SSTC), calls were made to reform and strengthen South-South and Triangular Cooperation as a tool for achieving the 2030 Sustainable Development Agenda.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements best describes
the ‘Transient lunar phenomena’?

options.a:
Rapid shifts in lunar albedo caused by Earth’s
atmospheric scattering
options.b:
Periodic brightening of lunar craters due to
reflection changes during perigee
options.c:
Short-lived luminous events such as brief
flashes and glows occurring on the lunar surface
options.d:
Periodic small-scale volcanic eruptions on
the Moon that release lava and ash

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Transient Lunar Phenomena (TLPs)** are **brief, unpredictable visual events** observed on the Moon’s surface, lasting from a few seconds to several hours.
● They include **sudden flashes of light,** reddish or coloured glows, hazy mists, or localized brightening, often reported in regions such as the **Aristarchus and Plato craters.** Though observed for over a **thousand years,** including by **Apollo 11 astronauts**, their origin remains uncertain.
● Leading theories include **outgassing of radon or argon** triggered by crustal stresses, **meteoroid impacts** producing momentary flashes, and **electrostatic levitation** of lunar dust. Some sightings may also arise from **distortions in Earth’s atmosphere.** TLPs suggest that the **Moon is more dynamic** and active than previously believed. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** For centuries, sky-gazers have reported seeing peculiar, short-lived luminous events on the moon’s surface known as transient lunar phenomena (TLPs).

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
With reference to mud volcanoes, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 They are generally found near tectonic
subduction zones.
II. 	 The Baratang Island volcano is the only mud
volcano located in India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Mud volcanoes** are geological structures formed by the **eruption of mud, water**, and gases such as **methane**, **CO₂**, and **nitrogen**. Unlike true igneous volcanoes, they do not erupt lava; instead, they **expel** **cool mud slurries** driven by deep overpressured sediments, hydrocarbon activity, or tectonic deformation.
● Mud volcanoes frequently occur in regions experiencing **tectonic compression**, including **subduction** **zones**, **accretionary wedges**, and fault-controlled basins where **deep fluids** migrate upward. They can be found on **land or under the ocean.** **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Baratang Island**, around 100–150 km north of Port Blair, is situated in the North and **Middle Andaman** **district.** It is **India’s only collection of mud volcanoes**, 11 in total across the archipelago, 8 of which are on **Baratang** and **Middle Andaman**. **So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India’s only mud volcano at Baratang Island in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands erupted after remaining dormant for over two decades.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Air, Water, Noise Pollution, SLCPs, etc.
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following statements best
explains the presence of weak spots in the Earth’s magnetic field?

options.a: Variations in solar wind intensity periodically erode certain portions of Earth’s magnetosphere
options.b:
The asymmetric gravitational pull of the
Moon distorts Earth’s internal magnetic structure
options.c:
Residual magnetism locked in the lithosphere
interferes with the geomagnetic field in certain regions
options.d:
Uneven circulation of molten iron–nickel in
the outer core leads to irregular magnetic flux distribution

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Earth’s magnetic field** is generated deep within the planet through the **geodynamo**, a process driven by the **turbulent motion** of electrically conducting **molten iron and nickel in the outer core.**
● Because this fluid **does not move in a uniform**, **symmetrical manner**, the magnetic field it produces is also **uneven**. As a result, certain regions exhibit **stronger magnetic flux**, whereas others show dispersed or **weaker intensities**, creating what scientists call **“weak spots.”**
● The most well-known of these is the **South Atlantic Anomaly**, which recent European Space Agency Swarm satellite data shows has **expanded slightly since 2014.** These variations are normal expressions of the constantly shifting fluid core.
● They **neither threaten Earth’s protective magnetic shield** **nor** indicate an imminent **magnetic reversal.** Weak spots can migrate, shrink, or intensify over decades as part of natural geomagnetic variability. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recent findings from the European Space Agency’s (ESA) Swarm mission reveal that the South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA) — the region of weakest intensity in Earth’s magnetic field — has expanded by nearly
0.9% since 2014.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.microTopic: Environmental and Global Issues (Climate Change, Drought, ZBNF, WTO, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to the
Enforcement Case Information Report (ECIR):
I. 	 It serves as a formal internal record prepared
by the Enforcement Directorate upon receiving information about a suspected offence.
II. 	 It must be recorded before initiating any
investigation under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002.
III. 	Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS),
2023 mandates giving the accused a copy of the ECIR.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer**: **a** **Explanation**:
● The Enforcement Case Information Report (**ECIR**) is an **internal document** prepared by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) when it receives information or material indicating a potential offence under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA). The ECIR functions as a **formal internal record**, but **not** **as a statutory FIR**. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Under the PMLA, **neither the Act nor its Rules mandate** that an **ECIR must be registered before the** **initiation** of an investigation. The ED can begin inquiry or proceedings based on information received, even without a formally recorded ECIR. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The ECIR is **not governed by the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS)**, and courts have consistently held that **supplying a copy of the ECIR to the accused** is **not mandatory**. The Supreme Court has clarified that an ECIR is an internal ED document and **not equivalent to an FIR**, and need not be shared with the accused.  **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance**: Recently, the Enforcement Directorate booked 29 individuals, including actors, anchors, and television hosts, for promoting illegal betting applications.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Snow leopard:
I. 	 It is the least genetically diverse big cat
species in the world.
II. 	 It exhibits a higher inbreeding coefficient
compared to other big cats.
III. 	It’s population found in India accounts for
roughly fifteen percent of the global estimate.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**:
● Snow leopards have been identified as the **least genetically diverse big cat species**. Genetic analyses of the species have consistently shown **very limited variation in their DNA**, which makes populations **more** **vulnerable to long-term ecological changes**. **So,** **statement I is correct**.
● The inbreeding coefficient indicates the **probability that an individual has two copies of the same gene** **from a common ancestor**. Studies on population genetics show that snow leopards have a **higher** **inbreeding coefficient** compared to other big cats such as tigers and lions. This is mainly due to their fragmented, high-altitude habitats and limited opportunities for population mixing. **So,** **statement II is** **correct**.
● According to official studies like the Snow Leopard Population Assessment in **India** (SPAI) and government conservation estimates, India is home to **about  10 - 15% of the world’s snow leopards**. **So,** **statement III is correct**. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: A Stanford study warns of future risks because of the low genetic diversity among snow leopards.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the United Nations Security Council:
I. - All UN Member States are obligated to
accept and implement decisions adopted by
the Security Council.
II. - The Security Council presidency rotates monthly among its members in English
alphabetical order.
III. Every Security Council decision on
substantive matters requires at least eight
affirmative votes, including the votes of all
permanent members.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● All UN Member States are obligated to accept and implement decisions adopted by the Security Council. **Article 25 of the UN Charter** states that **all members must agree to accept and carry out the decisions** **of the Security Council**. This binding nature applies specifically to decisions taken by the Council, particularly on matters of international peace and security. **So, Statement I is correct.**
● According to the Provisional Rules of Procedure of the Security Council (Rule 18), the **presidency rotates** **each month** following the **English alphabetical order** of member states' names. Each member holds the presidency **for exactly one calendar month**. **So, Statement II is correct.**
● **Decisions on substantive matters require at least nine affirmative votes, including the concurring** **votes of all five permanent members. Article 27(3) of the UN Charter** specifies that substantive decisions need:
○ **At least 9 votes in favour (out of 15)**, and ○ **No veto by any of the P5** (China, France, Russia, UK, USA). ○ A negative vote by any permanent member constitutes a veto. **So, Statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: At a recent United Nations Security Council (UNSC) meeting, India stated that nations promoting cross‑border terrorism must face a “serious cost” targeting Pakistan.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Transport (Roadways, Railways, Waterways, Ports, Airways, Pipelines, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement-I:**
A double rainbow occurs when two distinct
coloured arcs appear in the sky instead of one.
**Statement-II:**
Double rainbows involves two internal reflections
of sunlight inside raindrops.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II is the correct explanation for
Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct,
but Statement II is **not** the correct explanation
for Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● The primary rainbow forms after **one internal reflection** of sunlight inside a raindrop, while the secondary rainbow is produced after **two internal reflections**, which increases the angle of deviation and produces the secondary arc. The **secondary rainbow** appears outside the primary rainbow at a larger angle (~50– 53° from the anti-solar point) compared to the primary (~40–42°). **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Each internal reflection** inside a raindrop causes some **loss of light intensity**, which makes the s**econdary rainbow fainter than the primary**. The second reflection also **reverses the order of colours**,
so in the secondary rainbow, red appears at the bottom and violet at the top. The reduced brightness and inverted colour sequence are characteristic features of the secondary rainbow. **So, statement II is correct.** **So, both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II is the correct explanation for Statement**
**I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Recently, Bengaluru experienced light evening showers, leading to a rare double rainbow sighting that delighted residents.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements: **Statement-I:** Standard athletics tracks require athletes to run in an anti-clockwise direction. **Statement-II:** The human body is naturally a little stronger or more balanced on the left side.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II is the correct explanation for Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct,
but Statement II is **not** the correct explanation for Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Athletes** around the world **run in an anti-clockwise direction** on tracks. One common reason is that the **human body is naturally a little stronger or more balanced on the left side**. For example, the **heart is** **on the left side**, and the left side of the body helps to keep balance while turning. Because of this, making **left turns feels easier and more natural** when running on a track. So it gives **better balance, control,** **and speed during left turns**. **So, statements I and II are correct.** **So, both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II is the correct explanation for Statement**
**I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
“Shanchol” vaccine is used for the prevention of
which one of the following diseases?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Shanchol is an oral, **inactivated cholera vaccine** developed by Shantha Biotechnics (Sanofi India) for active immunization against **Vibrio cholerae** O1 and O139 serogroups.
● It is administered in two doses, at least 14 days apart, to individuals aged one year and older. The vaccine is WHO-prequalified and widely used in cholera-endemic regions and outbreak responses, including in Guinea, Haiti, and Yemen. It also provides herd protection and offers short-term efficacy even with a single dose. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **Cholera** is an acute diarrheal infection caused by consuming food or water contaminated with the bacterium Vibrio cholerae.
● Most people infected with V. cholerae do not develop symptoms but can spread the bacteria through their faeces for 1–10 days. Symptoms appear 12 hours to 5 days after infection.
**Relevance:** The oral cholera vaccine Shanchol has once again received prequalification from the World Health Organization (WHO).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Security of Supply Arrangement (SOSA) signed between the USA and India:
I. - It is legally non‑binding and operates as a mutual understanding rather than a formal treaty.
II. - Both countries provide reciprocal priority support for goods and services promoting national defence.
III. India has become the 18th SOSA partner of
the United States.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Security of Supply Arrangement (SOSA)** signed between the US Department of Defence and India's Ministry of Defence on August 22, 2024, was described as a **bilateral, non-binding agreement** **operating based on mutual understanding** rather than a formal treaty with legal obligations. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● Under SOSA, both countries commit to providing **reciprocal priority support for goods and services** **that promote national defence**, addressing supply chain disruptions. The US uses its **Defence Priorities** **and Allocations System** (DPAS), while India has established a **voluntary Code of Conduct** for its firms. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **India became the 18th SOSA partner of the United States** with this agreement, joining prior partners to enhance defence industrial collaboration during global supply chain challenges. **So, statement III is** **correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India and the United States have signed a Security of Supply Arrangement (SOSA) along with a Memorandum of Agreement on the Assignment of Liaison Officers.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Aquatic Ecosystem and Life (Aquatic Ecosystem, Maritime Zones, Corals, Mangrove, Marine Vegetation)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Gangaikonda Cholapuram
temple, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It was built by Rajendra Chola I in the Nagara
style of temple architecture.
II. 	 It is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site
known as the “Great Living Chola Temples”.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Gangaikonda Cholapuram temple** was built by Rajendra Chola I in the 11th century. Its architectural style is **Dravidian**, similar to the Brihadeeswarar temple at Thanjavur, but with a more graceful and curvilinear vimana (tower). It is not stellate-shaped (star-shaped). Stellate-shaped temples are a characteristic feature of Hoysala architecture, as seen in the Chennakesava temple at Belur and the Hoysaleswara temple at Halebidu. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● The **temple is part of the trio of Great Living Chola Temples**, along with the Brihadisvara Temple at Thanjavur and the Airavatesvara Temple at Darasuram, **designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site** **in 1987** and expanded in 2004. **So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Prime Minister offered prayers at the ancient Shiva temple of Gangaikonda Cholapuram and also released a commemorative coin in honour of Rajaraja Chola.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
In which one of the following groups are all three
countries part of the Death (Golden) Triangle?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The Golden Triangle (Death Triangle) is a notorious mountainous region in Southeast Asia, spanning approximately 200,000 km², where the borders of **Thailand, Myanmar, and Laos** converge at the confluence of the Ruak and Mekong rivers. Historically infamous for opium and heroin production since the 1950s (now shifting to synthetic drugs like methamphetamine), it remains a major narcotics hub despite eradication efforts.
● Golden Crescent includes illicit opium production areas in Iran, Afghanistan, and Pakistan, and the “Golden Triangle” covers Myanmar, Thailand, and Laos.
● India has been seen as sandwiched between the Death (Golden) Crescent and the Death (Golden) Triangle. The country is being flooded with drugs, especially heroin and methamphetamine, from these two regions. Nearly 90 per cent of the world’s demand for these drugs is being met from these two regions. India is both a big market and a transit route for other countries. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the United States has named India, along with China, as a “**state actor**” for facilitating the direct and indirect supply of precursor chemicals and equipment used by drug traffickers, particularly in the production of illicit fentanyl.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World Report, 2025:
I. 	 It is jointly prepared by five UN agencies.
II. 	 Africa accounts for the highest number of
undernourished people in the world.
III. 	India has the world's highest number of
wasted children under five years of age.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World Report 2025 report is jointly prepared by **five UN** **agencies:** Food and Agriculture Organization, the International Fund for Agricultural Development, the United Nations Children’s Fund, the World Food Programme, and the World Health Organization, to monitor progress on **SDG 2 (Zero Hunger)** targets. **So, statement I is correct.**
● SOFI 2025 estimates that 638–720 million people faced hunger in 2024, representing 7.8–8.8% of the global population. Although there has been some recent improvement, hunger levels remain **higher than** **before the pandemic**, reflecting the lasting effects of COVID-19, inflation, and ongoing conflicts.
● According to SOFI 2025, **Asia has the largest share in terms of absolute numbers**, with approximately **323 million undernourished people**, which is higher than other regions. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● According to the SOFI report, **18.7 percent of Indian children** under the age of five suffered from wasting in 2024, the **highest rate in the world.** **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The recent SOFI 2025 report indicates that around 37 million children under the age of five in India are stunted, highlighting widespread chronic undernutrition.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Money (Evolution, Types, Digital Currency, Crypto, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Battery Passport System:
I. 	 It assigns a unique digital identity to EV
batteries, carrying data on their chemistry, performance, and lifecycle.
II. 	 It is a mandatory legal requirement for
importing batteries into India.
III. 	The European Union has recently introduced
a Battery Passport System.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● India’s proposed **Battery Passport System** is a digital mechanism designed to provide complete and transparent information **about electric vehicle (EV) batteries**. The system will digitally record each battery’s specifications, including its **source, composition, performance, lifecycle, and supply chain**. A battery passport serves as an Aadhaar identity of sorts, with every battery having a unique ID that provides all information on the product. **So, statement I is correct.**
● India does **not require a Battery Passport** for importing EV batteries. While the government is exploring global best practices for battery safety, there is **no legal mandate** requiring importers to submit a Battery Passport. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The **European Union** has officially introduced Battery Passports as part of its **2023 EU Battery** **Regulation**. It makes it **mandatory for every industrial or EV battery in EU** with a capacity of **over 2** **kWh** to have a battery passport. It will come into force on Feb 1, 2027. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India is set to roll out a “Battery Passport” system

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText: Recently discovered Time Rondeau Crystal (TRC)’ is a:

options.a:
State of matter that shows perfect periodic
motion in time with no randomness
options.b:
Material whose atomic arrangement changes
in space
options.c:
State of matter in which long-term regularity
and short-term randomness coexist in time
options.d:
Crystal structure that changes its shape in
both space and time

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **Time Rondeau Crystal (TRC)** is a newly discovered **phase of matter** that shows a special kind of order not in space, but in time. Unlike normal crystals which have atoms arranged in a repeating spatial pattern, a TRC shows **repeating behaviour over time.** What makes it unique is that it combines both **order and randomness**. In the short term, the system behaves irregularly, almost randomly. But when
observed over longer periods, a clear repeating pattern appears. This is why scientists describe it as having **short-term randomness but long-term regularity**. It is different from a time crystal, which shows perfectly regular timing like a clock. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Scientists have discovered a new phase of matter called a time rondeau crystal that shows an unusual kind of order in time, rather than in space like solids and liquids.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Which of the following measures have contributed
to the decline in Gross Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) of Public Sector Banks in India  in recent years?
I. 	 Implementation of the Insolvency and
Bankruptcy Code
II. 	 RBI’s Prudential Framework for early
recognition and time-bound resolution of stressed assets
III. 	Establishment of specialised stressed-asset
management verticals in Public Sector Banks
IV. 	 Reduction in pecuniary jurisdiction of Debt
Recovery Tribunals (DRTs)
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A non-performing asset **(NPA)** is a loan or advance for which the **principal or interest payment** **remained overdue** for a period of **90 days.** Gross Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) of Public Sector Banks (PSBs) have fallen sharply in recent years from **9.11% in 2021 to 2.58% in 2025**.
● The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code has changed how bad loans are dealt with. It forces **quick** **resolution** by transferring control of a defaulting company away from its promoters. It also prevents wilful defaulters from participating in the resolution. Bringing personal guarantors under IBC has further strengthened recovery. Because of these changes, banks are now able to **recover dues faster,** contributing significantly to the fall in NPAs. **So, point I is correct.**
● RBI’s framework requires banks to **detect stressed loans early and resolve them within a fixed** **timeframe. I**t also rewards early resolution and penalises delays. By forcing timely action, the framework prevents loans from slipping into the NPA category and **reduces overall stress in the system.**  **So, point** **II is correct.**
● Public Sector Banks created **dedicated teams and branches to monitor and recover bad loans** more aggressively. These units use focused follow-up, business correspondents, and the “feet-on-street” model to **speed up recovery**. This professionalised approach has improved tracking of defaulters and boosted recoveries, helping reduce NPAs. **So, point III is correct.**
● The government **increased** the pecuniary jurisdiction of Debt Recovery Tribunals (DRTs) from **₹10 lakh** **to ₹20 lakh**, not reduced it. Increasing the threshold allowed DRTs to focus on high-value cases, improving recovery efficiency. **So, point IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:**    Gross non-performing assets (NPAs) of public sector banks have reduced from 9.11% to 2.58% from March 2021 to March 2025.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
questions.questionText:
The Darfur region, recently seen in the news, is
located in:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Darfur region** is a large geographical and cultural region located in **western Sudan**. It stretches between **Kordofan in the east and Wadai in the west, reaching from the Libyan Desert in the north** **to the Al-Ghazāl (Gazelle) River** in the south.
● It is known for its vast plains, the volcanic **Marrah Mountains**, and a population made up of nearly **80** **tribes and ethnic groups**.
● Historically, Darfur was an independent sultanate until Sudan annexed it in **1916**. In recent years, Darfur has been frequently in the news due to conflict involving the **Rapid Support Forces (RSF)** and the Sudanese Army. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, a Sudanese coalition led by paramilitary RSF announced a parallel government.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
Which of the following criteria may be considered
for declaring a disease as a notifiable disease?
I. 	 High potential to cause rapid mortality
II. 	 Poses a significant risk of international spread
III. 	Must be recognised by the WHO as a global
threat
IV. 	 Shifting geographical spread
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **notifiable disease** is any disease that must be legally reported to government health authorities. This reporting helps the government track cases, detect outbreaks early, and take quick public health action. A disease is declared “notifiable” when timely and regular case information is necessary for prevention, control, and policy-making.
● One major criterion for declaring a disease as notifiable is its **severity** especially if it can cause **rapid** **death or serious complications.** Diseases with high fatality rates need immediate reporting. **So, point I** **is correct.**
● Diseases that can easily **cross borders** a**nd spread internationally,** such as SARS, avian influenza, or Ebola, are often made **notifiable**. International Health Regulations (IHR) also require countries to report such diseases for global surveillance. **So, point II is correct.**
● A disease does **not need WHO recognition to become notifiable**. Governments decide based on national needs, incidence, potential outbreaks, and socio-economic impacts. **So, point III is not correct.**
● If a disease shows **new patterns** such as rising cases, new variants, or shifting geographical spread, it may be declared **notifiable. So, point IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, a parliamentary committee recommended declaring cancer a notifiable disease in India.

The term Tylenol, recently seen in the news,
refers to:

options.a:
A new antiviral medicine developed to treat
influenza
options.b:
An opioid-based drug used for cancer
patients
options.d:
A vaccine used to prevent childhood
respiratory infections

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Tylenol is a medicine that has been used in the United States and other countries for **pain relief.** Recently, suspicion was raised regarding its safety during pregnancy, following unverified claims linking it to autism. The claims however, were rejected by health experts. Tylenol is manufactured by Kenvue and contains **acetaminophen**, a commonly used drug for reducing pain and fever. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the U.S. health chief says there is not enough data to show Tylenol causes autism.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Income Tax Act, 2025, consider the following statements:
I. - It introduces the concept of a Tax Year, replacing the Assessment Year and Previous Year.
II. - It consolidates all Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) provisions into a single section to ease compliance.
III. It provides a statutory definition of Virtual
Digital Assets.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Income Tax Act, 2025** introduces a streamlined and modernized framework for **direct taxation** in India. It focuses on **clarity, simplification, and improved compliance** through structural and procedural reforms. The Act is designed to enhance transparency, reduce litigation, and align with technological developments.
● One of the key simplifications under the Income Tax Act, 2025 is the introduction of the term **‘Tax Year’**,
which replaces the earlier confusing terms **Assessment Year and Previous Year.** The Tax Year simply refers to the 12-month financial year starting on 1st April. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Earlier, TDS provisions were scattered across many sections, making it difficult for taxpayers and professionals to navigate the law. The 2025 Act simplifies this by grouping all TDS-related rules under a **single section 393**. This improves clarity, reduces confusion, and streamlines compliance. **So, statement** **II is correct.**
● **The Act defines Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs), including cryptocurrencies and tokenized assets. T**he scope of Virtual Digital Assets has been broadened to cover any asset that holds value in digital form and operates using cryptographic ledger systems such as cryptocurrencies or similar technologies. **So,** **statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The new Income Tax Act, 2025, was passed recently.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Biostimulants in India:
I. - They help enhance nutrient uptake and stress tolerance in plants.
II. - Some biostimulants are derived from seaweed extracts and plant waste.
III. They fall under the fertiliser or pesticide
categories.
IV. They are specified in Schedule VI of the
Fertiliser Control Order, 1985.
Which of the statements given above is/are **not** correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Biostimulant** is a **substance or microorganism** or a combination of both whose primary function when **applied to plants, seeds or rhizosphere** is to **stimulate physiological processes** in plants and to enhance its **nutrient uptake, growth, yield, nutrition efficiency, crop quality and tolerance to stress**. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● Biostimulants work by **stimulating natural processes in plant**s, such as nutrient absorption, stress tolerance, and overall yield.  These substances are sometimes made from plant waste or seaweed extracts. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Biostimulants are **not** regulated under the **Insecticides Act, 1968.** That Act regulates pesticides and plant growth regulators (PGRs). Biostimulants were outside legal regulation for many years, which led to its misuse. Because of this, the government amended the **Fertiliser Control Order (FCO), 1985 in 2021**
and officially included biostimulants under it. This gave the government authority to approve, regulate, test, and set standards for biostimulant products. **So, statement III is not correct.**
● **The FCO classified biostimulants specified in Schedule VI of the FCO** in eight categories, including botanical extracts (as well as seaweed extracts), bio-chemicals, vitamins, and antioxidants. Every manufacturer or importer of a biostimulant shall make an application to the Controller of Fertilisers along with the requisite product information. **So, statement IV is correct.**
● The Agriculture Ministry set up the **Central Biostimulant Committee in 2021** for a five-year term. It is chaired by the Agriculture Commissioner. The committee’s role is to **advise** the government on **new** **biostimulant inclusion, setting standards, testing protocols, lab requirements, and other regulatory** **matters**. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:**  Recently, the Union Agriculture Minister has asked state Chief Ministers to stop the forced sale of nano-fertilisers or biostimulants with subsidised fertilisers.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText: Which one of the following best explains the term ‘dark store’, recently seen in the context of quickcommerce platforms?

options.a:
A retail outlet that operates both during the
day and night hours
options.b:
A warehouse stocked with goods that is used
only as storage and not open to the public
options.c:
A specialised store selling discounted items
returned by customers
options.d:
A temporary facility created for inventory
storage

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Quick commerce (q-commerce) companies** like Blinkit, Zepto and Swiggy Instamart deliver groceries and essentials within **10–30 minutes**. To achieve such fast delivery, they rely on **dark stores**. These are small, **well-stocked warehouses located close to residential areas.** These stores are **not open to the** **public** and exist **only to fulfil online orders** quickly and efficiently. Dark stores look similar to retail outlets but function solely as **backend warehouses** for rapid delivery. They store items like milk, snacks, vegetables, and daily essentials, and staff inside pick and pack orders as soon as they appear on the app. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Blinkit outpaced its food delivery unit Zomato in net order value in the first quarter (AprilJune) of 2025-26.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Kyoto Protocol, consider the
following statements:
I. - It is the first legally binding climate treaty requiring developed countries to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions.
II. - The United States never ratified the Kyoto Protocol.
III. It ended once the Paris Agreement was
adopted in 2015.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Kyoto Protocol** was negotiated and **finalised in 1997** under the UNFCCC. After several years of ratification efforts by countries, it officially **came into force in 2005**. It became the first legally binding climate treaty requiring developed countries to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions through structured, time-bound commitment. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **United States never ratified the Kyoto Protocol.** This meant the world’s largest historical emitter had **no legal obligation** under the treaty to reduce emissions. The refusal weakened the effectiveness of the Protocol. **So, statement II is correct.**
● A key misunderstanding for many years was that the Kyoto Protocol ended once the Paris Agreement was adopted. However, the ICJ has now clearly stated that the **Paris Agreement did not supersede or** **terminate the Kyoto Protocol.** Although the third commitment period was never created after 2020, the treaty itself was never abolished. **It remains legally in force** as international law. **So, statement III is** **not correct.**
● The ICJ ruling clarified that countries that signed and ratified the Kyoto Protocol remain **legally bound** **to fulfill its provisions**. Non-compliance with emission reduction commitments even from the first commitment period, may be considered an **internationally wrongful act**. **So, statement IV is not** **correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the ICJ clarified that the 1997 Kyoto Protocol is still in force and remains legally relevant.
